end of world model.

When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

Level 1: you have used 12 of about 22 target actions so far.

World model:
- The UP action resulted in `board_changed: False` - tiles didn't move
- Orange tiles at rows 15-16, cols 34-38 (overlapping target box bottom)
- Blue tiles at rows 17-19, cols 34-38
- Target box at rows 9-15, cols 33-39 with inner b pattern at rows 11-13
- Tiles appear blocked from moving further UP into target box
- Need to test other directions (DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT) to understand movement constraints

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Test DOWN movement
result = action(['DOWN'])
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")

# Check tile positions
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nTile area (rows 14-22, cols 30-45):")
for r in range(14, 23):
    print(f"Row {r}: {lines[r][30:45]}")

# Find tile positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
orange_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'O']
blue_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] > 5]

print(f"\nOrange tiles: {[(n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in orange_nodes]}")
print(f"Blue tiles (large): {[(n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in blue_nodes]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Test DOWN movement
result = action(['DOWN'])
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")

# Check tile positions
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nTile area (rows 14-22, cols 30-45):")
for r in range(14, 23):
    print(f"Row {r}: {lines[r][30:45]}")

# Find tile positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
orange_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'O']
blue_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] > 5]

print(f"\nOrange tiles: {[(n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in orange_nodes]}")
print(f"Blue tiles (large): {[(n['boundary'], n['pixels']) for n in blue_nodes]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
